Test Scenario: Button Functionality
Test Case Description: Verify that the "Retry Trip" button is enabled and displays the correct label styling for retry action.
Expected Result: The "Retry Trip" button should be enabled when applicable, with appropriate label text and style (green with white text) to indicate a retry action.

Test Scenario: Search Input Field
Test Case Description: Verify that the search input field accepts PO ID, Trip ID and CO ID and displays placeholder text "Search by PO ID,Trip ID and CO ID".
Expected Result: The input field should accept text and correctly display placeholder text, with a proper width and style as per the design.

Test Scenario: Pagination Control
Test Case Description: Verify that the pagination control displays the correct number of pages and allows navigation to the next/previous page.
Expected Result: The pagination molecule should correctly display the number of pages and allow navigation between pages with proper button styling for "Next" and "Previous".

Test Scenario: Empty State for Trip Status
Test Case Description: Verify that if no trip status is available, the table displays "Not Available" in the corresponding status column.
Expected Result: If no trip status exists, the status column should show "Not Available" with proper alignment and style, without causing UI layout issues.

Test Scenario: Responsive Design on Table
Test Case Description: Verify that the table displays correctly on different screen sizes, maintaining its structure and scroll behavior in responsive view.
Expected Result: The table should be responsive, with proper column widths and scroll behavior on smaller screens without breaking the layout.

Test Scenario: Delivery Location Information
Test Case Description: Verify that the "Delivery Location" column displays correct location details, ensuring consistent formatting for different locations.
Expected Result: The delivery location information should display in the correct format without overflow or text truncation, regardless of the length of the location name.

Test Scenario: Pagination Dropdown Selection
Test Case Description: Verify that the "Results per page" dropdown correctly updates the number of rows shown in the table and the pagination adjusts accordingly.
Expected Result: Selecting a different value from the "Results per page" dropdown should update the table with the specified number of rows, and the pagination should update correctly.
Comments: Sundram Bhardwaj  we are using "Rows" cc Mahesh Prajapati

Test Scenario: Error Handling for Missing Data
Test Case Description: Verify that when any column data is missing (e.g., "PO Details"), an appropriate message (e.g., "Not Available") is displayed without breaking the table layout.
Expected Result: If column data is missing for any row, "Not Available" should appear in the corresponding field, without affecting the table’s design or structure.

Test Scenario: Error Validation for Invalid Input
Test Case Description: Verify that when invalid data (e.g., incorrect PO ID, Trip ID and CO ID) is entered, an error message appears without breaking the layout.
Expected Result: If an invalid PO ID,Trip ID or CO ID or CO ID is entered, an error message should appear below the input field, without disrupting the form layout or other elements.
Comments: Sundram Bhardwaj not valid for now cc Mahesh Prajapati

Test Scenario: Accessibility: Screen Reader Support
Test Case Description: Verify that all icons and interactive elements (e.g., buttons, dropdowns) are accessible via screen readers with appropriate labels.
Expected Result: Icons and interactive elements should be readable by screen readers, with correct aria labels, allowing visually impaired users to navigate the UI effectively.

Test Scenario: Color Contrast Compliance
Test Case Description: Verify that all text, buttons, and interactive elements meet WCAG color contrast standards, ensuring readability for all users.
Expected Result: All text, buttons, and interactive elements should meet WCAG 2.1 AA color contrast guidelines, providing clear readability and visual separation for accessibility.

Test Scenario: Focus Indicator for Interactive Elements
Test Case Description: Verify that all interactive elements (e.g., input fields, buttons) display a clear focus indicator when navigated to via keyboard.
Expected Result: When navigating through the UI using the keyboard, a visible focus indicator (e.g., border or outline) should appear on input fields, buttons, and dropdowns.

Test Scenario: Character Limit Validation
Test Case Description: Verify that input fields (e.g., PO ID, Trip ID CO ID) enforce character limits of 150 character and show an error if the user exceeds the limit.
Expected Result: Input fields should not accept more characters than specified in their validation rules (e.g., maximum 150 characters for a Trip ID) and should show an error if exceeded.
Comments: Sundram Bhardwaj we have limt of 150 char but showing error is not mentioned in the figme cc Mahesh Prajapati

Test Scenario: Performance of Dropdown Loading
Test Case Description: Verify that dropdowns (e.g., "State") load their options quickly without noticeable delay, especially when dealing with large datasets.
Expected Result: Dropdowns should load options swiftly, even with a large number of options, ensuring a smooth user experience without long delays.

Test Scenario: Loading Indicator for Slow Operations
Test Case Description: Verify that when clicking a button that triggers a slow operation (e.g., data fetching), a loading spinner or indicator appears.
Expected Result: If an action triggered by a button takes time to complete (e.g., data fetch), a loading spinner or progress indicator should appear, informing the user of ongoing work.

Test Scenario: Cross-Browser Consistency
Test Case Description: Verify that all components (input fields, buttons, icons, dropdowns) render and function consistently across major browsers (Chrome, Safari).
Expected Result: All elements should render and function consistently across different browsers (Chrome, Safari, Firefox, Edge), with no visual or functional discrepancies.

3. Network Issue: If there's a network or other issue, a toast message pops up in red with a cross icon and the text "Unable to refresh the trip status. Please try again." in white.
